metadata.title: Agentic Research Course
metadata.author: Seyed Yahya Shirazi, Ph.D.
metadata.description: Week 2: Setting Up Claude Code for Research -- From Installation to Your First AI-Assisted Task

elements.content: Week 2: Setting Up Claude Code for Research

**Seyed Yahya Shirazi, Ph.D.**
Assistant Project Scientist, Swartz Center for Computational Neuroscience
UC San Diego

[Open Science Collective](https://osc.earth)

slides.speakerNotes:
- Welcome back; welcome to newcomers

- Last week: git, GitHub, terminal (the safety net)

- Today: install Claude Code, the tool that needs the safety net

- Week 1 recording on YouTube; link in Discord

- Course site: courses.osc.earth/agentic-research

- Discord link on the slide

elements.content: For those who missed last week, or need a refresher:

items.text: **git** = version control on your machine. Commits = snapshots you can return to
**GitHub** = online platform. PRs, code review, issues
**Terminal** = text interface. Where Claude Code lives
**`gh` CLI** = GitHub from the command line

elements.code:
# git: version control        # gh: GitHub from CLI
git add .                     gh issue create
git commit -m "update"        gh pr create
git push                      gh repo clone user/repo
git log                       gh issue develop 42

elements.content: Week 1 recording + setup guide available in the Discord and on YouTube

slides.speakerNotes:
[Press -> 6x to reveal all fragments]

- Audience grew 10x; give a 2-minute foundation

- git: tracks changes locally

- GitHub: online platform, enables collaboration

- Terminal: text interface where Claude Code runs

- gh CLI: GitHub from command line

- Key insight: AI is powerful -> QA and version control matter MORE, not less

elements.alt: Claude face icon with wink animation

elements.content: An agentic coding tool that reads, writes, runs, and iterates

slides.speakerNotes:
- Section divider: Meet Claude Code

- Let's get into the agent itself

items.text: Reads your **entire project** and understands the structure
Writes and edits code **across multiple files**
Runs terminal commands (build, test, lint, deploy)
Creates git commits, branches, and pull requests
Plans multi-step implementations **before writing a line**
Iterates on its own work until tests pass

elements.content: You describe what you want. Claude Code figures out how to build it.

slides.speakerNotes:
[Press -> 7x to reveal all fragments]

- Claude Code is NOT autocomplete or chat

- Autonomous agent that:
  - Reads entire project
  - Writes/edits across multiple files
  - Runs terminal commands
  - Creates commits, branches, PRs
  - Plans before writing code
  - Iterates until tests pass

- You describe the outcome; Claude figures out the implementation

elements.content: This course focuses on **Claude Code**. Cowork is Anthropic's newest agentic product (generally available) for non-coding tasks like spreadsheets and presentations.

slides.speakerNotes:
[Press -> 2x to reveal all fragments]

- Three products from Anthropic:
  - Claude Chat: conversational AI in browser
  - Claude Code: the coding agent we're learning
  - Claude Cowork: generally available, for non-coding agentic work (spreadsheets, presentations, documents)

- Cowork and Code both on Pro and above

- Course focuses on Code

elements.alt: Claude Code interface diagram showing Terminal, VS Code, Desktop, Web, JetBrains

items.text: **Terminal CLI** -- full-featured, what we teach in this course
**VS Code / Cursor** -- inline diffs, @-mentions, plan review
**Desktop App** -- visual diffs, multiple sessions, scheduled tasks
**Web** (claude.ai/code) -- no local setup, long-running tasks
**JetBrains** -- PyCharm, IntelliJ, WebStorm
**Third-party: Conductor** -- GUI wrapper, parallel agents, visual workflows

elements.content: All official surfaces share the same engine. Your CLAUDE.md, settings, and MCP servers work everywhere.

slides.speakerNotes:
[Press -> 7x to reveal all fragments]

- Claude Code runs everywhere:
  - Terminal CLI (what we teach)
  - VS Code / Cursor extension
  - JetBrains plugin
  - Desktop app
  - Web at claude.ai/code
  - Third-party: Conductor (macOS GUI wrapper)

- All official surfaces share same engine

- CLAUDE.md, settings, MCP servers work everywhere

- Left: VS Code extension (inline diffs, conversation panel)

- Right: Desktop app (visual diff review)

- Both connect to same engine

- Pick whatever feels comfortable

- Section divider: Getting Started

- Installation, auth, permissions

elements.code:
# macOS / Linux (recommended)
curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash

# macOS via Homebrew
brew install --cask claude-code

# Windows PowerShell
irm https://claude.ai/install.ps1 | iex

elements.content: Then run `cd your-project && claude` to start. Native install auto-updates. Homebrew/WinGet require manual upgrade.

slides.speakerNotes:
[Press -> 2x to reveal all fragments]

- Installation is one command

- Native install recommended (auto-updates)

- Homebrew works but upgrade manually

- Windows: use PowerShell (Git for Windows required for native)

- Then: cd your-project && claude

- First run: browser opens for login

rows: Auto-approves file edits + filesystem commands (mkdir, mv, cp, rm, sed)
Prevents edits; can still read and run commands with prompts

elements.title: Shift+Tab
elements.content: Press **Shift+Tab** to cycle Default -> Accept Edits -> Plan. Auto mode is opt-in via `--enable-auto-mode` and not available on Pro or Max.

slides.speakerNotes:
[Press -> 2x to reveal all fragments]

- Default: asks before edits and commands

- Accept Edits: auto-approves edits AND filesystem commands (mkdir, mv, cp, rm, sed)

- Plan: prevents edits; can still read and run commands (with prompts)

- Auto: opt-in via --enable-auto-mode; Team/Enterprise/API only, NOT Pro/Max

- Shift+Tab cycles Default -> Accept Edits -> Plan

- Start at Default, move up as trust builds

elements.content: The most important file in your project

slides.speakerNotes:
- Section divider: CLAUDE.md

- The most important file in your project

elements.content: A markdown file with **persistent instructions** that Claude reads at the start of **every session**.

items.text: You write it; it guides Claude's behavior
A good CLAUDE.md turns a generic AI into a **domain-aware research assistant**
Think of it as the briefing you'd give a new team member
Run `/init` to generate a starter, then refine

elements.alt: Screenshot of /init generating a CLAUDE.md file

slides.speakerNotes:
[Press -> 5x to reveal all fragments]

- CLAUDE.md = persistent instructions loaded every session

- Plain markdown at project root

- Turns generic AI into domain-aware assistant

- Run /init to generate a starter

- Then refine manually

- Think of it as onboarding a new team member

items.text: **Project CLAUDE.md** = team standards, check into git. **User** = personal, all projects.
**Local** (CLAUDE.local.md) = personal project-specific, gitignored. Subdirectory files load on demand.

slides.speakerNotes:
[Press -> 3x to reveal all fragments]

- Multiple scopes, more specific wins:
  - Managed (org-wide, IT-deployed)
  - Project (./CLAUDE.md, shared via git)
  - User (~/.claude/CLAUDE.md, personal)
  - Local (./CLAUDE.local.md, gitignored)

- All are concatenated, not override

- Subdirectory files load on demand

elements.content: ## What Goes In vs. What Stays Out

items.text: Build and test commands
Code style rules (non-default)
Repo etiquette (branch naming, PR conventions)
Architecture decisions
Common gotchas
"Never do this" rules
Things Claude can infer from code
Standard language conventions
Detailed API docs (link instead)
Info that changes frequently
Long tutorials
Self-evident practices

elements.content: **Keep under 200 lines.** Bloated CLAUDE.md files cause Claude to ignore your actual instructions. If Claude follows a rule without the instruction, delete it.

slides.speakerNotes:
[Press -> 13x to reveal all fragments]

- From official Anthropic best practices

- Include: build commands, code style rules, workflow conventions, architecture decisions, gotchas, never-do rules

- Exclude: things Claude can infer, standard conventions, long tutorials, frequently-changing info

- CRITICAL: keep under 200 lines

- If Claude ignores a rule, file is too long

- If Claude already does something right without the rule, delete the rule

elements.content: For larger projects, split instructions into topic files:

elements.code:
.claude/
├── CLAUDE.md              # Main instructions
└── rules/
    ├── code-style.md      # Code style guidelines
    ├── testing.md          # Testing conventions
    └── security.md         # Security requirements

elements.content: Rules can be **path-scoped** with YAML frontmatter:

elements.code:
---
paths:
  - "src/api/**/*.ts"
---
# API Rules
- All endpoints must validate input
- Use standard error response format

elements.content: Path-scoped rules only load when Claude works with matching files, saving context space.

slides.speakerNotes:
[Press -> 4x to reveal all fragments]

- For larger projects: split rules into .claude/rules/*.md

- Each file covers one topic (testing, security, code-style)

- Powerful: path-scoped rules via YAML frontmatter

- Only load when Claude works with matching files

- Keeps context clean

- We use this pattern in research-skills templates

elements.content: If your repo uses AGENTS.md for other tools (OpenAI Codex, other agent frameworks), import it:

elements.code:
# CLAUDE.md
@AGENTS.md

## Claude Code Specific
Use plan mode for changes under src/billing/.

elements.content: The `@path` syntax imports files inline. Works with any file: `@README.md`, `@package.json`, `@docs/conventions.md`

slides.speakerNotes:
[Press -> 4x to reveal all fragments]

- AGENTS.md is NOT read by Claude Code

- Some repos use it for OpenAI Codex, other agent frameworks

- Solution: import into CLAUDE.md with @ syntax

- Both tools read same instructions, no duplication

- @ import works with any file (README, package.json, etc.)

elements.content: Plugins, Skills, MCP, Hooks, and Agents

slides.speakerNotes:
- Section divider: Extending Claude Code

- Plugins, Skills, MCP, Hooks, Agents

elements.alt: Plugin ecosystem: Plugin contains Skills, Hooks, Agents, MCP. Below: CLAUDE.md and Auto Memory always loaded.

slides.speakerNotes:
[Press -> 1x to reveal all fragments]

- Plugin = distributable package

- Inside a plugin:
  - Skills (workflows, load on demand)
  - Hooks (automated actions at lifecycle points)
  - Agents (isolated workers, own context)
  - MCP servers (external tool connectors)

- Below: CLAUDE.md + auto-memory always loaded

items.text: Stored in `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md`
Claude loads them **on demand**, not every session
Invoke with `/skill-name` or Claude auto-detects from context
Can include supporting files: templates, scripts, examples

rows: Run a prompt repeatedly on an interval
Load Claude API reference for your language

elements.content: **Skill vs CLAUDE.md:** CLAUDE.md = facts loaded every session. Skills = procedures loaded on demand. If CLAUDE.md is getting long, move procedures to skills.

slides.speakerNotes:
[Press -> 7x to reveal all fragments]

- Skills = SKILL.md files

- Load on demand, NOT every session

- Invoke with /skill-name or Claude auto-detects

- Can include supporting files (templates, scripts, examples)

- Bundled skills: /simplify, /batch, /debug, /loop, /claude-api

- Skill vs CLAUDE.md:
  - CLAUDE.md = facts every session
  - Skills = procedures on demand

- If CLAUDE.md grows, move procedures to skills

elements.content: **Model Context Protocol** -- an open standard for connecting AI to external data sources.

elements.code:
# Add an MCP server
claude mcp add notion-server npx @notionhq/mcp-server

# Or configure in .mcp.json

items.text: **Serena:** semantic code exploration (LSP-powered symbol navigation)
**LSP servers:** real-time code intelligence for TypeScript, Python, Rust, Go
**hed-mcp:** Hierarchical Event Descriptor (HED) annotation for neuroimaging
**matlab-mcp-tools:** drive EEGLAB / MATLAB from Claude Code
**General:** Google Drive, Notion, Jira, Slack, databases

elements.content: Our practicum uses **matlab-mcp-tools** to drive EEGLAB from Claude Code for EEG analysis.

slides.speakerNotes:
[Press -> 8x to reveal all fragments]

- MCP = Model Context Protocol (open standard)

- Research-relevant MCPs:
  - Serena: semantic code exploration
  - LSP servers: real-time code intelligence
  - hed-mcp: HED annotation for neuroimaging
  - matlab-mcp-tools: drive EEGLAB from Claude Code (our practicum)

- General: Google Drive, Notion, Jira, Slack, databases

- Add with: claude mcp add <name> <command>

elements.content: Automated actions at lifecycle points. **Deterministic**, not advisory.

items.text: Run linter after every file edit
Block destructive commands
Auto-format code on save
Inject context at session start

elements.content: Isolated workers with their **own context window**.

items.text: Keeps main conversation clean
Good for research / exploration
Can restrict tools and model
Returns summary, not raw data

elements.content: **Hooks:** "This WILL happen." | **CLAUDE.md:** "Please do this." | **Agents:** "Do this in your own space."

slides.speakerNotes:
[Press -> 9x to reveal all fragments]

- Hooks = automated actions at lifecycle points
  - Deterministic, not advisory (unlike CLAUDE.md)
  - Examples: run linter after edits, block dangerous commands, auto-format

- Agents = isolated workers, own context window
  - Keep main conversation clean
  - Good for research / exploration
  - Can restrict tools and model
  - Return summary, not raw data

- Three-way distinction:
  - Hooks: "this WILL happen"
  - CLAUDE.md: "please do this"
  - Agents: "do this in your own space"

elements.content: ## Plan Mode: Think Before You Code

elements.alt: Plan mode workflow: Explore, Plan, Implement, Commit

items.text: **Explore** (Plan Mode): Claude reads files, builds understanding
**Plan** (Plan Mode): Claude proposes approach, you review
**Implement** (Normal Mode): Claude writes the code
**Commit** (Normal Mode): Claude commits and opens PR

elements.content: Enter with **Shift+Tab** (twice) or `/plan`. Press **Ctrl+G** to edit the plan in your editor.

elements.alt: Screenshot of Claude Code in plan mode

slides.speakerNotes:
[Press -> 4x to reveal all fragments]

- Most important feature for complex changes

- Shift+Tab twice to enter

- Four phases: Explore -> Plan -> Implement -> Commit

- Claude explores read-only, proposes plan

- You review plan (Ctrl+G opens in editor)

- Approve, then Shift+Tab back to normal to implement

- Prevents solving the wrong problem

- Skip for small changes (one-liner fixes, renames)

rows: Generate starter CLAUDE.md from your codebase

elements.title: Key shortcuts
elements.content: **Shift+Tab** = cycle permission modes | **Esc** = stop Claude | **Esc+Esc** = rewind | **Ctrl+G** = open in editor | **@** = file autocomplete | **!** = bash mode

slides.speakerNotes:
[Press -> 1x to reveal all fragments]

- /init bootstraps CLAUDE.md

- /plan for exploration before implementation

- /clear between unrelated tasks (critical for context)

- /compact summarizes when context fills

- /model to switch Opus/Sonnet/Haiku

- /rewind and Esc+Esc for undo

- Shortcuts: Shift+Tab (modes), Esc (stop), @ (file autocomplete), ! (bash mode)

elements.content: ### 1. Give Claude a way to verify its work
Include tests, expected outputs, or screenshots. This is the **single highest-leverage** thing you can do.
### 2. Explore first, then plan, then code
Separate research from implementation. Use Plan Mode for the thinking phase.
`/clear` between tasks. Use subagents for investigation. After two failed corrections, start fresh with a better prompt.

slides.speakerNotes:
[Press -> 3x to reveal all fragments]

- Direct from Anthropic's official best-practices page

- #1: Give Claude a way to verify
  - Tests, expected outputs, screenshots
  - Highest-leverage thing you can do

- #2: Explore, plan, THEN code
  - Don't jump to implementation
  - Use Plan Mode for exploration

- #3: Manage context aggressively
  - /clear between tasks
  - Subagents for research
  - After 2 failed corrections, /clear and rewrite prompt

elements.content: Opinionated additions on top of official Claude Code features

slides.speakerNotes:
- Section divider: Our Research Workflow

- Our opinionated additions on top of official features

elements.content: Four files that track your project's evolving state across sessions:
Claude reads these files. Your `plan.md` becomes its task list. Your `scratch_history.md` prevents it from repeating your mistakes.

slides.speakerNotes:
[Press -> 1x to reveal all fragments]

- NOT an official Claude Code feature

- Our opinionated pattern for project state

- Four files in .context/:
  - plan.md: current task list
  - ideas.md: design decisions
  - research.md: technical findings
  - scratch_history.md: failed attempts (maybe the most valuable)

- Claude reads all of them

- scratch_history prevents repeating mistakes

elements.content: Free, open-source Claude Code plugin for academic research workflows:

slides.speakerNotes:
[Press -> 1x to reveal all fragments]

- Free, open source companion plugin

- Seven domains, mapped to course weeks:
  - project: /init-project, /epic-dev, /setup-ci
  - opencite: literature search
  - grant: proposal writing and review
  - manuscript: peer review, formatting
  - scientific-figures: publication figures
  - neuroinformatics: BIDS, PsychoPy
  - presentation: these slides

- Install with correct marketplace suffix: @research-skills

items.text: Most US universities are **501(c)(3) nonprofits** -- the lab registers as the nonprofit unit
Nonprofit Team plan is **for orgs under 20 people** ($8/seat); larger groups route to Enterprise
Verification through Goodstack (~3 minutes)
**Education plans** available for university-wide deployment; contact Anthropic
For this course: **Pro ($20/mo or $17 annual)** is sufficient

elements.content: PIs: if your lab has fewer than 20 people at a 501(c)(3) university, apply as the nonprofit unit for Team seats at **$8/seat/month** via Goodstack.

slides.speakerNotes:
[Press -> 6x to reveal all fragments]

- Pro at $20/mo ($17 annual) is enough for this course

- Big news for PIs:
  - Most US universities are 501(c)(3) nonprofits
  - Labs under 20 people: apply as nonprofit unit
  - Team plan drops from $25 to $8/seat/month
  - Goodstack verification: ~3 minutes

- Above 20 people: Enterprise

- University-wide: contact Anthropic Education team

- API-heavy: Console account, workspace spend limits

items.text: Install Claude Code and verify it works (`claude --version`)
Run `/init` in a project to create your first CLAUDE.md
Create the `.context/` directory with `plan.md`, `ideas.md`, `research.md`, `scratch_history.md`
Ask Claude Code to do one task related to your research
Review what it generated and refine the CLAUDE.md manually
(Optional) Install the research-skills plugin

elements.content: **Next week:** Project management with AI -- epics, sprints, worktrees, and structured development workflows.

slides.speakerNotes:
[Press -> 7x to reveal all fragments]

- Homework checklist:
  - Install Claude Code
  - Run /init to generate CLAUDE.md
  - Create .context/ directory
  - Give Claude ONE real research task (small is fine)
  - Review what it generated, refine CLAUDE.md
  - Optional: install research-skills plugin

- Next week: project management (epics, sprints, worktrees)
